sections.paragraph:
νεόφυτος, ον (νέος, φύω) Aristoph., Fgm. 828 I p. 581 Kock; PRyl 138, 9 [34 a.d.]; BGU 563 I, 9 al. [Dssm., NB 47f—BS 220f]; Ps 127:3; 143:12; Is 5:7; Job 14:9; JosAs 25:2) lit. ‘newly planted’, fig. (only in Christian lit.) newly planted in the Christian community, newly converted (cp. ‘neophyte’) 1 Ti 3:6.—DELG s.v. νέος C1. M-M.

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Kock Kock , s. Com.—List 5

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

BS BS , s. Dssm., BS—List 6

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 669
